mappings.rationaleAI: The cell covers Robert Hooke's discovery, Schleiden and Schwann's cell theory, organelle structure and function (nucleus, mitochondria, ribosomes), cell division (mitosis and meiosis), and endosymbiosis -- spanning cell theory, classification, design, function, and the cell cycle.
Macromolecules -- nucleic acids, proteins, polysaccharides, and lipids -- are the molecular system components of cells, directly mapping to the cell as a molecular system.
Mitochondria as organelles with independent DNA and double-membrane structure are key morphological elements of cells, directly relevant to cell organelle morphology.
Genetic mutations including chromosomal rearrangements relate to cell division errors during meiosis, relevant to the division of germ cells into gametes with potential genetic alterations.
Free radicals' role in cellular damage and cell signaling relates to the internal environment and cell matrix, as oxidative stress affects the intracellular milieu.

Slime moulds demonstrate remarkable cell movement through aggregation and their transition between unicellular and multicellular states illustrates cells in combination forming multicellular organisms.
